compiled_state.title: TDD RED phase produces change-detector tests instead of behavioral assertions
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: PROBLEM: The TDD RED phase in /dso:implementation-plan and /dso:tdd-workflow generates tests that assert 'this string exists in this source file' rather than 'this behavior produces this outcome.' These tests pass when the implementation text is present and fail when it's absent, regardless of whether the code actually works. They are change-detector tests — they detect edits to source code, not behavioral correctness.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: ROOT CAUSE: During TDD RED phase, the implementation doesn't exist yet. The agent writes a test for the not-yet-implemented feature. For shell scripts and skill files, the agent defaults to the easiest assertion: grep the source file for the expected string/function/pattern. This passes the RED requirement (test fails because the string doesn't exist yet) and turns GREEN when the implementation is added (string now exists). But the test never actually exercises the behavior — it only checks that the code was written.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
INVESTIGATION FINDINGS — Change-detector tests by file:

test-merge-to-main-qt4u.sh: 14 tests, ALL grep-based. Uses sed -n to extract function bodies then greps for strings like 'git rm', 'rebase --continue', 'archive'. Zero behavioral assertions. The sed ranges use fragile echo-string sentinels (dso-t2wb).

test-merge-to-main.sh: ~8 grep-based tests checking for CLI arg handling, parallelism patterns, function existence. Tests 1-4 (parallel validation) grep for '&', 'wait', PID patterns in source. Test 5 was a flaky timing test (w20-51ti, now fixed with PID comparison).

test-ci-status-auth-ratelimit.sh: 6 tests grepping ci-status.sh for 'gh auth', 'rate.limit', 'MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS'. None actually run ci-status.sh.

test-plugin-retro-gather.sh: 6 tests grepping retro-gather.sh for config patterns, artifact prefixes, completion markers.

test-nohup-cleanup.sh: 5 tests grepping for 'cleanup_stale_nohup', 'workflow-nohup-pids' in hook files.

test-project-setup-*.sh (3 files): ~15 tests grepping SKILL.md for prompt counts, CI generation patterns, dependency explanations.

Skill frontmatter tests (test-fix-bug-skill.sh, test-init-skill.sh, test-generate-claude-md-skill.sh): grep for headings, keywords, step numbers in SKILL.md files.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
COMMON PATTERNS observed across all change-detector tests:

1. grep -c PATTERN source.sh then assert_ne 0 — checks string exists in file
2. grep -q PATTERN source.sh — same but boolean
3. sed -n '/START/,/END/p' source.sh | grep PATTERN — extract section then check string
4. awk '/^function_name/{f=1} f{print}' source.sh | grep PATTERN — extract function body then check
5. grep -v '#' source.sh | grep -c PATTERN — exclude comments then count occurrences

None of these execute the script under test, create test fixtures, or verify output/behavior.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
IMPACT: (1) False confidence — tests pass but behavior is untested. Example: test-merge-to-main-qt4u.sh passed for months while the v2-only patterns it checked were stale. (2) Fragile maintenance — sed sentinel ranges break on refactoring (dso-t2wb). String counts break when code moves between functions. (3) Flaky failures — timing-based behavioral proxies like the sleep-and-measure parallelism test (w20-51ti) fail under load. (4) Oscillation risk — fixing a fragile test by adjusting the grep pattern is a surface fix that doesn't address the missing behavioral coverage.

FIX NEEDED IN TWO PLACES: (1) The TDD RED phase guidance in /dso:implementation-plan and /dso:tdd-workflow needs to explicitly prohibit grep-the-source-file assertions for behavioral requirements. RED tests must exercise the code under test, not scan its text. (2) Existing change-detector tests (~70 across 15 files) should be triaged: delete tests that duplicate behavioral coverage from integration tests, replace tests for untested behavior with real integration tests, keep tests that enforce legitimate structural constraints (no relative paths, config migration, frontmatter validation).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral. Dual-signal: ticket describes red-test-writer agent behavior + affected file is agents/red-test-writer.md.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: SAFEGUARD APPROVED: user approved editing plugins/dso/agents/red-test-writer.md. Fix: narrow architectural contract exception, strengthen anti-change-detector guidance.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
